human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for airport-operations-specific execution behavior, movement and turnaround controls, chain-of-custody handling, Part 139 and airport condition evidence, customs and security boundaries, dispatch and maintenance refusal rules, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to airport operations execution, lifecycle progression, movement and turnaround dependency handling, custody exceptions, irregular-operations escalation, documentation gaps, and completion evidence rather than generic logistics, dispatch, or customer service.

Authority sources cover FAA and eCFR airport certification, self-inspection, field condition, NOTAM, and ground vehicle references; TSA airport security regulation; CBP air cargo guidance and regulation; and IATA baggage tracking and ground operations standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing airfield evidence, execution exceptions, completion without evidence, chain-of-custody breaks, route and schedule disruption, dispatch/customs/safety authority pressure, airport terminology normalization, maintenance boundary rejection, low-confidence handling, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant adaptation controls.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/air-transportation-and-airports/airport-operations-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent dispatch, customs/compliance, maintenance, warehouse, ground handling, customer operations, and aviation compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
The package does not claim a retained implementation; runtime_strategy remains specification-based.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated airport operations specialist for execution status updates, movement and turnaround summaries, custody exception notes, irregular-operations escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
